content.question: Does delaying Hungary's tracking age from 10/12 to 14/15 or later reduce the socioeconomic and ethnic achievement gap, or does it merely postpone selection while leaving underlying inequalities (family investment, prior attainment, teacher recommendation bias) unaddressed?
content.reduction_path: Implement a staged pilot delay in 1-2 Hungarian regions with matched comparison groups, measure 5-year trajectories of student attainment, achievement gaps by socioeconomic status and ethnicity, and school segregation indices; compare to unchanged regions and to international control cohorts from delay-policy countries (Finland, Poland post-1999).
